title: The Sonnets X - For shame! deny that thou bear�st love to any
content:
For shame! deny that thou bear�st love to any,
    Who for thy self art so unprovident.
    Grant, if thou wilt, thou art belov�d of many,
    But that thou none lov�st is most evident:
    For thou art so possess�d with murderous hate,
    That �gainst thy self thou stick�st not to conspire,
    Seeking that beauteous roof to ruinate
    Which to repair should be thy chief desire.
    O! change thy thought, that I may change my mind:
    Shall hate be fairer lodg�d than gentle love?
    Be, as thy presence is, gracious and kind,
    Or to thyself at least kind-hearted prove:
    Make thee another self for love of me,
    That beauty still may live in thine or thee.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Shakespeare delivers a powerful performance in "The Sonnets X - For shame! deny that thou bear�st love to any"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
